storefront_description: The storefront combines blue-teal corrugated metal mural panels with a recessed entry protected by a diamond-pattern security gate, producing a graphic but still compact street-level retail face.

facade_description: 205 Augusta Ave presents a converted house-form, detached facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 3 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its converted house-form, detached typology. The facade reads primarily as mixed (corrugated metal lower, dark cladding upper) in a blue/teal corrugated metal with tree mural at ground, dark cladding upper palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 1, 1 window bays from lower to upper levels, using fixed, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include cornice. HCD note: The property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that has been modified to accommodate a commercial conversion through ground floor alterations. Through its construction date and typology, the property is associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s t
